titleStmt.title: Letter from Gerald Blue to Sir Robert Chalmers, 15 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Gerald Blue

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

correspAction.placeName: Department of Recruiting for Ireland, 4th Area Office, Mansion House, Dublin, Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Department of Recruiting for Ireland 4th AREA OFFICE MANSION HOUSE DUBLIN. Telephone No. 4838.
I5th. May I916. Sir Robert Chalmers, K.C.B.
Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle. Sir,
I have the honour to submit for your consideration an
application for the position of Secretary to the Committee about to
be formed in connection with the damage to the City.
For a number of years I acted as Fire Loss Assessor to
a number of the leading Fire Insurance Offices in Dublin and have
a wide experience of the settlement of claims connected with all class
classes of trades.
Shortly after the outbreak of war I engaged in Recruiting
work in various parts of Ireland and since the inception of this
Office have acted as Organiser for the City and County of Dublin
under the Sub Directo for the Area, Alderman James Moran.
I have considerable experience of secretarial work and
enjoy the confidence of a number of influential business men in
the City.
I have the honour to be, Sir,
Your obedient servant,
Gerald Blue
